question_text: What does בראשית mean in this pasuk?
student_explanation: In this pasuk, בראשית means in the beginning.

metsudah_translation_support: In the beginning Elohim created the heavens and the earth.
source_support_note: The exact Hebrew target בראשית is present in the verse, and both translation sources support the same classroom-level meaning.

question_text: What does המים mean in this pasuk?
student_explanation: In this pasuk, המים means the waters.
hebrew_context: וְהָאָ֗רֶץ הָיְתָ֥ה תֹ֙הוּ֙ וָבֹ֔הוּ וְחֹ֖שֶׁךְ עַל־פְּנֵ֣י תְה֑וֹם וְר֣וּחַ אֱלֹהִ֔ים מְרַחֶ֖פֶת עַל־פְּנֵ֥י הַמָּֽיִם׃
koren_translation_support: And the earth was without form and void; and darkness was on the face of the deep. And a wind from God moved over the surface of the waters.
metsudah_translation_support: The earth was unformed and desolate, and (there was) darkness over the surface of the abyss. The breath of Elohim hovered above the surface of the water.
source_support_note: The exact Hebrew target המים is present in the verse, but Koren and Metsudah vary slightly in English wording, so the item is flagged for review.
dikduk_rule_name: Recognize plural-looking nouns that often translate as singular ideas in English

hebrew_context: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר אֱלֹהִ֗ים יְהִ֤י מְאֹרֹת֙ בִּרְקִ֣יעַ הַשָּׁמַ֔יִם לְהַבְדִּ֕יל בֵּ֥ין הַיּ֖וֹם וּבֵ֣ין הַלָּ֑יְלָה וְהָי֤וּ לְאֹתֹת֙ וּלְמ֣וֹעֲדִ֔ים וּלְיָמִ֖ים וְשָׁנִֽים׃

hebrew_context: וַיְבָ֧רֶךְ אֹתָ֛ם אֱלֹהִ֖ים לֵאמֹ֑ר פְּר֣וּ וּרְב֗וּ וּמִלְא֤וּ אֶת־הַמַּ֙יִם֙ בַּיַּמִּ֔ים וְהָע֖וֹף יִ֥רֶב בָּאָֽרֶץ׃
koren_translation_support: And God blessed them, saying, Be fruitful, and multiply, and fill the waters in the seas, and let birds multiply in the earth.
metsudah_translation_support: Elohim blessed them saying, Be fruitful and multiply, fill the waters of the seas, and let the birds multiply on the earth.
source_support_note: The exact Hebrew target ורבו is present in the verse, and both translation sources support the same classroom-level meaning.
dikduk_rule_name: Identify prefixed ו as 'and'
dikduk_rule_summary: A ו at the beginning of a noun often means 'and'.
student_error_pattern_summary: Student missed prefixed ו as a conjunction.

question_text: Which English order sounds natural for ויאמר אלהים?
student_explanation: Hebrew says the clause in a different order, but natural English here is God said.
hebrew_context: וַיֹּ֥אמֶר אֱלֹהִ֖ים יְהִ֣י א֑וֹר וַֽיְהִי־אֽוֹר׃
koren_translation_support: And God said, Let there be light: and there was light.
metsudah_translation_support: Elohim said, Let there be light. and there was light.
source_support_note: The exact Hebrew target ויאמר is present in the verse, and both translation sources support the same classroom-level meaning.
dikduk_rule_name: Reorder Hebrew word order naturally in English translation
dikduk_rule_summary: When Hebrew word order sounds unnatural in English, translate the meaning naturally instead of copying the order mechanically.
student_error_pattern_summary: Student copied Hebrew order mechanically instead of translating the clause naturally.
why_this_question_was_generated: This question was kept because ויאמר is instructionally valuable, but it needs teacher review before any locked subset because the wording or support is more delicate.
what_the_question_tests: This tests whether the student can translate the clause naturally rather than copying Hebrew order mechanically.
why_the_correct_answer_is_correct: The exact Hebrew target ויאמר appears in the pasuk, and both Koren and Metsudah support the keyed answer 'God said'.

why_each_distractor_is_wrong.said God: said God: This choice is not supported by the Hebrew target ויאמר in this pasuk.
why_each_distractor_is_wrong.God light: God light: This choice is not supported by the Hebrew target ויאמר in this pasuk.
why_each_distractor_is_wrong.light said God: light said God: This choice is not supported by the Hebrew target ויאמר in this pasuk.

teacher_review_note: Use only if the teacher wants students to practice natural English reordering explicitly.

question_text: Which noun is ותוצא matching in this clause?
student_explanation: הארץ
hebrew_context: וַתּוֹצֵ֨א הָאָ֜רֶץ דֶּ֠שֶׁא עֵ֣שֶׂב מַזְרִ֤יעַ זֶ֙רַע֙ לְמִינֵ֔הוּ וְעֵ֧ץ עֹֽשֶׂה־פְּרִ֛י אֲשֶׁ֥ר זַרְעוֹ־ב֖וֹ לְמִינֵ֑הוּ וַיַּ֥רְא אֱלֹהִ֖ים כִּי־טֽוֹב׃
koren_translation_support: And the earth brought forth grass, herb yielding seed after its kind, and tree yielding fruit, whose seed was in itself, after its kind: and God saw that it was good.
metsudah_translation_support: The earth brought forth grass, herbs yielding seed of its kind, and trees bearing fruit which has in it seeds of its kind; and Elohim saw that it was good.
source_support_note: The exact Hebrew target ותוצא is present in the verse, and both translation sources support the same classroom-level meaning.
dikduk_rule_name: Match verb agreement to noun gender and number
dikduk_rule_summary: The verb should match the noun in gender and number.
student_error_pattern_summary: Student is not yet using gender as an active grammar clue.
why_this_question_was_generated: This question was kept because ותוצא is instructionally valuable, but it needs teacher review before any locked subset because the wording or support is more delicate.
what_the_question_tests: This tests whether the student can track what ותוצא is doing in the clause.
why_the_correct_answer_is_correct: The exact Hebrew target ותוצא appears in the pasuk, and both Koren and Metsudah support the keyed answer 'הארץ'.

why_each_distractor_is_wrong.המים: המים: This is a different nearby creation noun, not the target meaning.
why_each_distractor_is_wrong.האור: האור: This is another creation noun, but it is not the meaning or function of the tested word.
why_each_distractor_is_wrong.הלילה: הלילה: This is another creation noun, but it is not the meaning or function of the tested word.

teacher_review_note: Check that subject-verb agreement has already been introduced before using this with learners.
